name: Movie.2020.BD
reason: RE2 '$' matches only at end of text; .NET '$' also matches before a final newline, so .NET sees BD(?!$) fail on a name ending in '\n' while the layer matches. Accepted RE2 core-syntax contract (R3.4): release names never carry newlines, and the provider-boundary clamp keeps the layer's documented semantics byte-oriented.

name: 7s-Series-s02e01-720p.mkv
reason: Overlapping-candidate handling: the leading '-Series-s02e01' candidate is rejected (its lookahead sees 720p ahead at every legal shrink width), and RE2's non-overlapping scan resumes after the rejected span, so the layer's next candidate is '-720p' where .NET rescans from the failed start+1 and finds '-s02e01-720p'. Rescanning inside rejected spans would make per-call work quadratic on adversarial input, violating the R3.7 measured linear-time gate; accepted as a documented contract.
